title: Watcher state is memory-only - every daemon restart re-fires file/email watchers as if events were new
source: Claude Fable improvement pass 2026-07-07 - noted in _check_email's own docstring ('no persistence beyond a restart'); becomes user-visible noise now that restarts are about to become routine (post-landing restart, T-284 staleness alerts)
current_state: WatcherManager._state (agent/watchers.py:308) - the per-watcher dict holding seen email ids, file mtimes, url hashes, keyword line counts, price was_triggered - lives only in process memory. watchers.db persists the watcher definitions but not their state. On every daemon restart: the email watcher re-alerts for all currently-unread mail (up to 10, bundled), file watchers fire on the first post-restart change with a stale baseline, url/keyword watchers re-baseline silently, and price watchers re-alert if past threshold (the T-277 edge-trigger resets). One restart = a burst of duplicate Telegram pings.
target_state: Watcher state survives restarts: add a state TEXT column to the watchers table (idempotent ALTER TABLE migration exactly like the existing analyze-column migration at _init_db line ~89), load it into self._state on the first sweep, and persist json.dumps(new_state) in the same UPDATE that already writes last_check each sweep. Tests: create a watcher, run one sweep with state-producing config (tmp file), construct a SECOND WatcherManager on the same db, assert its first sweep does NOT re-fire for the unchanged file; and assert the email evaluator's seen_ids survive the reconstruction (fake gmail, same message ids -> no fire).
migration_plan: 1. Tests first in testing/test_watchers_v2.py: the two reconstruction cases above using _make_wm(tmp_path) twice on the same db path.
2. _init_db: idempotent 'state' column migration (copy the analyze-column pattern at line ~89).

3. _sweep: on first encounter of a wid not in self._state, seed it from the row's state column (json.loads, default {}); extend the existing last_check UPDATE to also write state=json.dumps(new_state).
4. python scripts/verify.py must print PASS (never pipe, T-214); SOLUTIONS.jsonl; close; refresh_pi.py.
risk_notes: agent/ is NOT in SAFE_COMPONENTS - hand/Sonnet ticket, not sprint fodder. Keep state opaque JSON per watcher; do not normalize it into columns (each evaluator owns its own shape). The per-sweep UPDATE already happens - piggyback on it, do not add a second write. If json.loads of a stored state fails, fall back to {} and track_silent.
